key_insight: The paper treats each attention token as a bare matrix Lie group element and computes attention from the intrinsic relative pose log(g_i^{-1}g_j), giving an equivariant, closed-form Lie-algebra kernel without learned representation machinery.
engineering_relevance: This offers a parameter-efficient attention mechanism for geometric sequence modeling on matrix Lie groups, including non-compact affine transforms with scale and shear, where standard vector-token or irrep-based methods struggle.
method: Token = group element g_i, not a feature vector
Score pairwise similarity with s_ij = -||log(g_i^{-1}g_j)||^2 / τ under a block-weighted Frobenius norm
Use the canonical relative transform log(g_i^{-1}g_j) to get intrinsic equivariance on any chart where the logarithm is defined
evidence_signal: The abstract reports sequence-completion experiments on SE(2), SO(3), and Aff(2), where the closed-form score matches a learned MLP kernel, outperforms it on SE(2), uses 50–80× fewer score parameters, and preserves invariance far better than a vector-token baseline.
watch_item: The method depends on choosing a logarithm chart that contains the relevant relative poses, so chart coverage and numerical stability near singular regions are practical implementation concerns.
paper_title: The Token Is a Group Element: On Lie-Algebra Attention over Matrix Lie Groups
